name: Kelantan (Malaysia)

colors.symbolism: the loyalty of the rakyat (people) to the Sultan
the purity and sovereignty of the Sultan

description: A red field charged in the centre with a white circular emblem containing a crescent and star surrounded by spears, lances, a kris and traditional Malay royal symbols, all in red.
history: The state flag of Kelantan, north-eastern Peninsular Malaysia. The central emblem is the seal of the Sultan, derived from royal regalia of the Kelantan Sultanate.

features.role: central white disc bearing royal regalia — crescent, star, kris, spears, and umbrellas
features.symbolism: the seal of the Sultan of Kelantan, depicting his royal regalia
